title: Discrete pit-window hazard priors
summary: Pit decisions are naturally a per-lap hazard with stint resets, not just an independent row classification problem.
rationale: Pit decisions are naturally a per-lap hazard with stint resets, not just an independent row classification problem. F1 strategy references describe optimal stint length as a function of degradation and pit-loss, while recent pit-stop research uses both next-lap classification and remaining-laps-to-stop signals.
implementation_hint: From training only, create OOF smoothed hazard tables by Race x Compound x Stint x binned TyreLife, RaceProgress, and Estimated_Laps_Remaining. Add features such as historical next-lap pit rate, cumulative survival since stint start, and distance to modal pit lap for each race/compound/stint bucket.
expected_effect: Should improve AUC by sharpening ranking around common pit windows while preserving the current strong continuous tyre features.
risk: Sparse buckets and train-test year drift can overfit; use KFold/GroupKFold out-of-fold encoding with strong smoothing and fallback global priors.
